title: Communicate loading progress, not just loading appearance
summary: AI-generated loading states default to a spinning animation or shimmer skeleton screen without communicating progress expectations, estimated duration, or what is being loaded.
instruction: Replace generic loading indicators with progress information that helps the user wait: estimated time, step indicators for multi-phase loads, or meaningful content previews. Consider skeleton screens only when the proportions match the actual content.
rationale: A spinning indicator communicates nothing about progress or expected duration. Users cannot estimate whether a wait will be 2 seconds or 20 seconds. The shimmer skeleton often uses incorrect proportions, misrepresenting the eventual content layout.
violations: Generic spinning circle or rotating icon as the only loading indicator
Skeleton screen with gray rectangular blocks and left-to-right shimmer animation
Skeleton proportions that do not match the actual content dimensions
Spinner centered both horizontally and vertically regardless of content position
Identical loading animation timing parameters across all contexts
controls: Progress steps with labels for multi-phase operations
Content-aware skeleton that matches actual layout proportions
Progressive content reveal instead of an all-at-once loading screen
borderlineCases: Operations where progress is genuinely unpredictable (AI inference, file transcoding)
contextualExceptions: Brand-consistent loading animation that is part of the visual identity
detectorSignal: A centered animated element (rotating icon or pulsing blocks) that appears temporarily during data fetching, with no progress or timing information.
matcherProposal: Detect animated SVG/CSS spinner elements or a container of gray rectangular blocks with shimmer animation that precede content rendering.
detectorLimitations: Cannot distinguish between meaningful loading feedback and decorativs spinner
falsePositiveScenarios: Quick operations where progress communication would add more delay than it saves
mitigationStrategy: Focus detection on loading states that persist for more than a threshold (e.g., 2 seconds) without communicating progress.

fixturePlans.violation: Centered spinning circle on brand color, no progress info, appears for 3+ seconds.
fixturePlans.control: Progress bar with 'Loading your dashboard...' label and estimated time for slow operations.
fixturePlans.borderline: Quick-loading action that shows a brief spinner for <500ms with no additional info needed.

cleanRoomAttestations: Developed without access to proprietary prompt collections.
overlapNotes: Related to motion.transition-all but addresses loading communication specifically.
